title: lathyrus venosus subsp. arkansanus
intro: Lathyrus venosus subsp. arkansanus, also known as the Arkansas sweet pea or sweet pea, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Lathyrus venosus subsp. arkansanus include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
